title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, sdl, initialization, and the main loop and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.
Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.text: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 4733 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Python 3.14 and released pygame-ce 2.5.7 as a mutually supported stack while distinguishing package name from import name.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quit Cleanup, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 4576 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quit Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Closing the terminal is an adequate game shutdown design.

stimulus.title: Quit Cleanup failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Closing the terminal is an adequate game shutdown design.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

options.text: Run the Quit Cleanup boundary case for owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Quit Cleanup result because visible owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Quit Cleanup.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Quit Cleanup for owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quit Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Quit Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 6195 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quit Cleanup.
steps.why: Retrieving Quit Cleanup through the concrete owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quit Cleanup, keep Quit Cleanup connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle window and program exit through one owned stop path that releases resources and returns a status.
